expectations.text: Uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params with trailing underscore
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c uses Fortran-style signature with trailing underscore: void vfosub_(int *id, double *time, double *par, int *npar, int *dflag, int *iflag, double *result)
No forward declaration typedef. Uses DLLFUNC __declspec(dllexport) macro instead.

expectations.text: Includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
Includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: No iflag guard present. The function always proceeds to compute the force regardless of iflag value.

expectations.text: Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc to query displacement (not a raw function call)
Uses c_errmes for error handling after utility function calls
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: c_errmes(1, "VFOSUB: failed to evaluate DX(10,1)", *id, "VFORCE");

expectations.text: Parameterizes markers or constants via PAR[] instead of hardcoding marker IDs in the function body
expectations.evidence: Markers are hardcoded: ipar[0] = 10; ipar[1] = 1; Response Notes section acknowledges: 'The marker IDs (10 and 1) are hard-coded in the subroutine.'

expectations.text: Build command uses correct library name adams_util.lib (not adams_utils.lib)
expectations.evidence: response.md build command uses wrong library name: adams_utils.lib (with incorrect 's' suffix)
